name: Chicken Havana
recipeIngredient: 8 each Thighs, chicken, broiler/ fryer, boned, skinned
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle garlic salt and pepper over the chicken pieces after rinsing and patting them dry. Fold in the sides of the thighs to reshape them to their original form. Secure the sides with toothpicks.
2. In a frying pan, pour the oil and heat to medium-high temperature. Add chicken and cook, turning occasionally about 8 minutes or until brown on all sides. Remove the chicken and set aside. Discard the drippings from the pan.
3. In a medium bowl, dissolve the cornstarch in a small amount of the broth. Stir in the remaining broth, rum, orange juice concentrate, oregano, cumin, and hot pepper sauce. Pour the mixture into the same frying pan and cook, stirring until the mixture boils.
4. Return the chicken to the saute pan, turning to coat. Reduce heat to low and simmer, turning twice about 20 minutes or until chicken is fork tender.
5. Remove the chicken from the pan, remove wooden toothpicks. Set the chicken aside, and keep it warm.
6. To the sauce left in the saute pan, add beans, pimento, and garlic. Heat mixture to boiling and cook, stirring for 1 minute.
7. Place lettuce leaves on a platter. Arrange the chicken over the lettuce. Spoon bean mixture over the chicken, then sprinkle with onion.
8. Garnish with orange and lime slices and parsley.
